source.description: Six-panel contact sheet generated with the process-first workflow, then cropped into step frames and the finished image.
source.notes: Chosen as an honest early archive backfill with a clear spring fruit subject and a different drawing problem from recent jars, flags, beach objects, and garden tools.

process_strategy: A six-panel process builds the basket from a shallow oval and woven body, then adds strawberries, leafy caps, handle, texture, restrained color, and final keeper lines.

final_elements.name: shallow basket body
final_elements.description: A low oval basket with curved sides and a stable base.

final_elements.name: basket rim and handle
final_elements.description: A thick oval rim plus a simple arched handle rising above the basket.

final_elements.name: visible strawberries
final_elements.description: Several rounded heart-like strawberries tucked inside the basket.

final_elements.name: leafy strawberry caps
final_elements.description: Small leafy caps on the berries, kept simple and readable.

final_elements.name: basket weave lines
final_elements.description: Light crisscross weave marks following the basket curve.

final_elements.name: strawberry seed dots
final_elements.description: Tiny seed marks on the existing berries.

final_elements.name: red and green pencil color
final_elements.description: Restrained red berry color and green cap accents.

frames.visible_job: Places the shallow oval basket body, side curve, and base guide.
Adds the thick basket rim and arched handle connected to the sides.
Adds the main strawberry shapes inside the basket.
Adds leafy berry caps and basket weave marks on existing shapes.
Adds seed dots, restrained red berry color, green caps, and a small ground shadow.

final_step.visible_job: Only strengthens the basket, handle, berries, caps, seed dots, weave, existing color, and ground shadow.

rejection_checks: No new berry, handle, basket wall, weave system, leaf cap, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
The basket must keep the same angle, scale, and berry arrangement in every frame.
The finish must stay a casual colored-pencil sketch, not a glossy food illustration.
